column1: How do you grant an EC2 instance permissions to access AWS resources without managing credentials?
column2: Create a custom IAM policy and attach it to the instance via an IAM role (instance profile)

column1: Do security groups grant permissions to access AWS resources?
column2: No, security groups control network access; permissions are granted by IAM

column1: Can two instances in different VPCs have the same private IP address?
column2: Yes, because private IP address ranges can be assigned to multiple VPCs

column1: What S3 storage class is best for data accessed less frequently but requires rapid access when needed?
column2: S3 Standard-IA (Infrequent Access) - offers lower storage costs with rapid access capability

column1: What is the fastest retrieval time for S3 Glacier?
column2: Typically 3-5 hours (not suitable when retrieval in minutes is required)

column1: What is the best AWS service for storing session state in a distributed architecture?
column2: DynamoDB - provides scalability, high availability, durability, and is fully serverless

column1: Is Redshift suitable for session storage?
column2: No, Redshift is a data warehouse for analytical queries, not transactional session storage

column1: What is the purpose of RDS read replicas?
column2: Enhance performance and durability by serving read traffic from multiple copies of data, increasing aggregate read throughput

column1: Does a multi-AZ RDS deployment increase read throughput?
column2: No, the secondary instance is only for failover and does not serve requests alongside the primary

column1: What happens to RDS automatic backups when the database is deleted?
column2: Automatic backups are not retained after deletion; you must take a manual snapshot to preserve data

column1: What type of DynamoDB index allows you to use a different partition key and sort key?
column2: Global secondary index (local secondary index can only have a different sort key)

column1: What causes an eventually consistent read to return outdated data in DynamoDB?
column2: Eventually consistent reads may not return the most up-to-date data immediately after a write

column1: What is DynamoDB Local?
column2: A downloadable version of DynamoDB that enables local development and testing without accessing the web service

column1: Is encryption of Amazon EBS volumes mandatory?
column2: No, encryption of EBS volumes is optional

column1: Can Elastic Beanstalk automatically modify IAM policies attached to service roles?
column2: No, Elastic Beanstalk cannot make automated changes to policies attached to service roles and instance roles

column1: What happens if a revision does not pass a manual approval in CodePipeline?
column2: It is treated as a failed revision, but successive revisions can still progress past the approval gate if approved

column1: Are S3 bucket names globally unique?
column2: Yes, S3 bucket names are globally unique and cannot be changed after creation

column1: What happens if you manually update a CloudFormation-managed resource outside of CloudFormation?
column2: Creates inconsistencies between CloudFormation's expected state and actual resource state, causing future stack operations to fail

column1: What is the correct order for processing streaming data with SQL queries in AWS?
column2: Kinesis Data Streams → Kinesis Data Analytics (for SQL queries) → Kinesis Data Firehose

column1: What DynamoDB feature publishes messages when table data changes?
column2: DynamoDB Streams - publishes a message every time there is a change in the table

column1: Does Lambda support DynamoDB Streams as an event source?
column2: Yes, Lambda can poll DynamoDB Streams and trigger functions based on table changes

column1: What HTTP methods does API Gateway support?
column2: GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE, HEAD, and OPTIONS

column1: Can you access the underlying infrastructure of AWS Lambda?
column2: No, Lambda is a managed service running in containers; you cannot access the underlying infrastructure

column1: What are two caching services available in AWS?
column2: ElastiCache and CloudFront

column1: What API Gateway feature allows you to create separate paths for development and production?
column2: Stages - enable separate paths with multiple endpoints

column1: What authorization methods does API Gateway support?
column2: IAM policies, Lambda custom authorizers, and Amazon Cognito user pools

column1: What tool allows you to test Lambda functions locally?
column2: AWS SAM CLI (Serverless Application Model Command Line Interface)

column1: What Cognito feature stores user profile information like username and phone number?
column2: Cognito user pools

column1: What caching engines does Amazon ElastiCache support?
column2: Redis and Memcached

column1: What does the CloudWatch alarm state INSUFFICIENT_DATA mean?
column2: The alarm hasn't had sufficient time to collect data or needs a minimum number of polling periods before becoming active

column1: What EC2 instance type provides a balance of compute, memory, and networking resources with burstable CPU?
column2: T2 instances - low-cost with CPU that can burst when additional cycles are available

column1: Which Savings Plan payment option offers the largest discount?
column2: All Up-front payment option

column1: What is the maximum compressed size of a Lambda deployment package?
column2: 50 MB compressed, 250 MB uncompressed

column1: What EBS volume type is best for transaction-heavy, IOPS-intensive database workloads?
column2: io1 (offers up to 64,000 IOPS on Nitro-based instances)

column1: What is the maximum IOPS for gp3 EBS volumes?
column2: 16,000 IOPS

column1: What DynamoDB partition key design causes request throttling?
column2: Few possible values (like status codes) lead to uneven data distribution and throttling

column1: What is a key benefit of serverless architecture?
column2: No server management, no compute costs during idle time, instant scaling to meet demand

column1: What is the maximum execution time for a Lambda function?
column2: 15 minutes per execution

column1: Which Aurora option is serverless and MySQL-compatible?
column2: Aurora Serverless (MySQL-compatible)

column1: Is Neptune suitable as a replacement for a relational database?
column2: No, Neptune is a graph database for highly connected datasets, not a relational database replacement

column1: What AWS service provides a prebuilt sign-up and sign-in UI?
column2: Amazon Cognito

column1: Can S3 host a static website?
column2: Yes, S3 can be configured to host static websites

column1: What is the minimum memory allocation for a Lambda function?
column2: 128 MB

column1: What is the maximum size of all code and dependencies for a Lambda function?
column2: 250 MB, plus an additional 10 GB of ephemeral storage in the /tmp directory

column1: What is the default visibility timeout for SQS?
column2: 30 seconds

column1: What are the two types of keys you can use in DynamoDB?
column2: Partition key and sort key

column1: What is ElastiCache?
column2: A managed in-memory caching service supporting Redis and Memcached

column1: What AWS service provides metrics for Lambda performance?
column2: CloudWatch provides performance metrics for Lambda

column1: What ECS launch type allows running containers without managing EC2 instances?
column2: Fargate launch type

column1: What AWS service can automatically rotate secrets on a schedule?
column2: AWS Secrets Manager

column1: What AWS service allows running SQL queries directly on data stored in S3?
column2: Amazon Athena (uses standard ANSI SQL)

column1: What AWS service provides scalable NFS-compatible shared storage for EC2 instances?
column2: Amazon EFS (Elastic File System)

column1: What is AWS AppSync used for?
column2: A managed service for creating and managing GraphQL APIs for web and mobile backends

column1: Does API Gateway only support RESTful APIs?
column2: No, API Gateway also supports WebSockets and custom HTTP interfaces

column1: What AWS resource acts as a firewall for EC2 instances and RDS databases?
column2: Security groups (not traditional firewalls with centralized allow/deny lists)

column1: What is the primary purpose of IAM roles?
column2: Provide temporary security credentials for AWS services to access resources securely

column1: What database engines are compatible with Amazon Aurora?
column2: PostgreSQL and MySQL (not Oracle or SQL Server)

column1: What does an EC2 instance type determine?
column2: System-level resources such as CPU and RAM (not OS, CPU architecture, or pricing model)

column1: What Elastic Beanstalk deployment policy creates new instances before switching traffic?
column2: Immutable deployment policy (creates new Auto Scaling Group, switches traffic only when healthy)

column1: What happens to EC2 instance store data when you stop and start an instance?
column2: Instance store data is lost because stopping may relocate the instance to different hardware

column1: What happens to EBS volumes when an EC2 instance is stopped?
column2: EBS volumes persist and remain attached (they stick around until explicitly detached)

column1: What AWS service stores and versions container images?
column2: Amazon ECR (Elastic Container Registry)

column1: What is the relationship between ECR and ECS?
column2: ECR stores container images; ECS pulls images from ECR for container orchestration
